emails.subject: you won't believe what Vernon did
emails.content:
Tomas,

So yesterday Vernon sends me this email asking me to put together a "team capacity overview" for Q4 planning. He wants hours worked, overtime, site assignments, scheduling gaps. The whole thing.

I've been running this crew for 12 years. I KNOW the capacity. I schedule these people every single week. But now I have to write it all up in a report like I'm justifying my job to someone who's been a supervisor for three months.

And the email had numbered action items. Numbered! Like I'm a new hire who needs step by step instructions. "Can you put together the following." He even said upper management wants to see it, so now its not just him reading it, its whoever he reports to.

I told him I'd put it together because what else am I going to say. But it took everything in me not to write back "I've been doing this since before you moved out of the commercial division."

Ayy I just needed to tell someone.

Lucia

emails.subject: Re: you won't believe what Vernon did
emails.content:
Hermana,

I hear you. That numbered action items thing is something else. Reminds me of when Carlos at the school district used to make us fill out request forms for light bulbs. Like we were going to steal light bulbs.

But mija here's the thing, he's new and he's nervous. Three months in and he's probably worried his boss is going to ask what he's been doing. So he overcompensates with reports and data and numbered lists so it looks like he's managing.

Doesn't make it less annoying ya sabes. Just means it's probably not about you. You've been there 12 years and everybody there knows you run that crew. He'll figure that out or he won't.

Do the report. Keep your head down. And call me if you need to yell about it some more.

Tomas

Vernon sent an email today saying he needs us to export all our CRM client notes for an annual review. ALL of them. Every engagement note, call log, meeting summary for the whole year.

Do you know whats in my CRM notes? Everything. When Darren Oshiro's daughter started at UH Manoa. Karen Lee's son's wedding plans. Brad Fujimoto talking about his retirement. I write all of that down because thats how I keep the relationships going. 12 years of knowing these people and yeah I keep track of their lives.

emails.subject: Re: now he wants our crm notes

Second you are 100% right to filter that out. Vernon wants a report for management not a diary of client relationships. Give him the work stuff. Site visits, call logs, project updates. Leave out everything personal.

Pues its going to be a pain going through all of it but mija just think of it this way. The personal notes are what make you good at your job. The report is just paperwork. Do the paperwork and keep the good stuff for yourself.

Tomas

emails.subject: lol fitbit sent me my year in review
emails.content:
Tomas,

Happy new year! Fitbit sent me one of those year in review emails today. Pretty cool actually. It says I did over 2 million steps last year and rode 247 miles on my bike.

But you know what caught my eye. It says I had 18 days where I walked less than 2,500 steps. And I know exactly what those days were. Every single one was a flare up day when the vog was bad or I got hit with dust at the jobsites. The fitbit literally has a record of every time my asthma knocked me out.

It also shows my resting heart rate spiking on those same days. So its not just the steps, its the heart rate too. Anyone looking at this thing could see a pattern real easy.

I know nobody else is going to look at my fitbit stats but it still makes me nervous after the whole calendar thing with Vernon. He was already asking about gaps in my schedule and now theres data that lines up perfectly with those same dates.

The cycling stuff is great though. Remember I told you about the charity ride in February? 22 miles! And all those training rides with Keoni in December. It says December was my most active month which makes sense because we were riding every Saturday.

Anyway just wanted to share. Happy new year hermano.

Lucia

emails.subject: Re: lol fitbit sent me my year in review
emails.content:
Hermana,

Happy new year mija!

Ok first of all 2 million steps and 247 miles on a bike at 59 years old. Most people our age are watching TV ya sabes. Thats something to be proud of.

Second about the low activity days. I hear you and I understand why it worries you especially after the Vernon thing. But mija Vernon does not have access to your fitbit. He sees your calendar and your work schedule. Your fitbit is your personal device on your personal account. He cant see those numbers unless you show them to somebody.

The only thing I would say is be careful about sharing that year in review stuff. Like if you screenshot it and send it to friends, anyone can see those low days and the heart rate spikes. Just share the cycling part and leave the rest for yourself.

247 miles hermana. Thats something.

Tomas
